history.qwen_rationale: Adding the jaxb-runtime dependency explicitly resolves the missing JAXB classes required for compilation on Java 11+. This allows the migration to proceed to Java 17 and 21.

history.qwen_observation: The build failed at java8_to_java11 with NoClassDefFoundError: javax/xml/bind/JAXBException, caused by JAXB removal in JDK 11 and an outdated maven-compiler-plugin (3.8.0) that cannot resolve the missing classes.
history.qwen_rationale: Explicitly adding javax.xml.bind:jaxb-api, org.glassfish.jaxb:jaxb-runtime, and javax.activation:javax.activation-api restores the JAXB classes required for compilation on JDK 11+. Upgrading maven-compiler-plugin to 3.11.0 ensures the compiler plugin itself is compatible with modern JDKs and avoids internal classloading failures.

history.qwen_observation: Build failed on JDK 11 with NoClassDefFoundError for javax.xml.bind.JAXBException during compilation, and maven-compiler-plugin remained at 3.8.0 despite the upgrade recipe.
history.qwen_rationale: JAXB modules were removed in JDK 11, causing compilation to fail when the compiler or annotation processors reference them. Explicitly adding jaxb-api and jaxb-runtime with compile scope ensures they are available on the classpath. Upgrading maven-compiler-plugin to 3.11.0 resolves known JDK 11 compatibility issues present in 3.8.0.

This follows the recommended trajectory of walking the framework up before bumping the JDK.

history.qwen_rationale: The maven-compiler-plugin 3.11.0 upgrade in step 1 is incompatible with JDK 11 execution and unnecessary. I will remove it, use 3.10.1 for JDK 11, simplify JAXB dependencies to avoid activation conflicts, and insert a Spring Boot 3.2 upgrade step under JDK 17 to satisfy the compatibility matrix and automatically handle the javax-to-jakarta migration before proceeding to JDK 21.

history.qwen_rationale: Skipping JDK 11 avoids the JAXB patching dead-end. Retaining the JAXB dependencies directly addresses the NoClassDefFoundError during compilation on JDK 11+.

history.qwen_observation: The build failed at the Java 17 bump step with `java.lang.NoClassDefFoundError: javax/xml/bind/JAXBException` during compilation, caused by the removal of JAXB modules in JDK 11+ and the `maven-compiler-plugin` (v3.8.0) failing to pass JAXB dependencies to annotation processors.
history.qwen_rationale: Moving the JAXB dependency additions to the Spring Boot 2.7 upgrade step ensures they are resolved before the JDK change. Explicitly upgrading `maven-compiler-plugin` to 3.11.0 in the same step forces Maven to use a version that correctly handles annotation processor classpaths on JDK 17+. Setting the scope to `runtime` avoids potential compile-time conflicts while still making the classes available to the compiler/processors. Removing the duplicate `AddDependency` calls from the JDK bump step keeps the chain clean.

history.qwen_observation: The build failed during the Spring Boot 3.2 upgrade step because the ehcache dependency was migrated to the Jakarta classifier but lost its version property, causing a Maven resolution error.
history.qwen_rationale: Spring Boot 3.x migration recipes often switch ehcache to the 'jakarta' classifier for namespace compatibility. When the version is stripped or not managed by the parent BOM for that classifier, Maven fails. Explicitly setting the ehcache version to 3.10.8 (compatible with SB 3.2 and Jakarta EE 10) in the upgrade step will resolve the missing version error and allow the build to proceed.
